mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding capacity utilization and rising manufacturing profitability, which lifts aggregate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following monthly INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50 percent of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 0.25 percent removes emergency monetary support, raising short-term discount rates and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as liquidity-sensitive assets reprice lower.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns after FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25 percent are negative in fewer than 55 percent of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in payroll employment and a declining unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that drives wage cost pressures, compresses corporate profit margins, and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in months where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously are not significantly negative compared to baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, an acceleration in consumer price inflation forces equity investors to apply higher discount rates to future cash flows, reducing present values and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during tightening cycles with rising CPIAUCSL are negative in fewer than 55 percent of triggered instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,500 thousand units indicates a contraction in residential construction activity and forward-looking demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as cyclical earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST readings below 1,500 are negative in less than 55 percent of occurrences over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real GDP reflects broad-based aggregate demand expansion that translates directly into higher expected corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following quarterly GDPC1 growth are not positive in more than 50 percent of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above 5 percent confirms recessionary labor market deterioration, triggering broad risk-off repositioning by investors and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns after UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent are negative in fewer than 55 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production and retail sales indicates an overheating goods sector where supply cannot match demand, sparking input cost inflation fears and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as margin compression dominates pricing.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in months where both INDPRO and RSAFS increase are not significantly negative versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.
